items.title: VI Live: PEC Zwolle begint goed aan duel met Ajax
items.summary: Ajax neemt het zondagmiddag om 12.15 uur op tegen PEC Zwolle. De ploeg van Fred Grim won één van de laatste vier competitieduels en speelde er drie gelijk. Tegen PEC hoopt Ajax op de volle buit. Of dat lukt, volg je in deze VI Live.

items.title: 'Ommekeer Vinícius: toch weer gesprekken met Real Madrid'
items.summary: We zitten in een periode tussen twee transferwindows, maar de stroom aan transfernieuws houdt nooit op. Traditiegetrouw volgt Voetbal International de buitenlandse markt op de voet. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Kane eyes Lewa goal record after Klassiker brace
items.summary: Harry Kane is targeting Robert Lewandowski's Bundesliga record of 41 goals in a season after notching another two in Bayern Munich's 3-2 win over Borussia Dortmund on Saturday.

items.title: Pep condemns boos during Ramadan fasting break
items.summary: A first-half stoppage allowing players who are observing Ramadan to break their fast was booed by sections of Leeds United supporters at Elland Road.

items.title: USMNT starlet Banks considering Germany switch
items.summary: U.S. youth national team starlet Noahkai Banks, a potential pick for this summer's senior World Cup squad, has said he is still considering whether his international future lies with the U.S. or Germany.

items.title: Barça's Yamal after 1st hat trick: I'm happy again
items.summary: Lamine Yamal has revealed he has not felt good this season but says he is finally enjoying football again after scoring the first hat trick of his career

items.title: Sources: Thorns hire ex-Spurs coach Vilahamn
items.summary: The Portland Thorns have hired Robert Vilahamn as head coach, multiple sources confirmed to ESPN, after a lengthy search.

items.title: Arsenal face another title test and buildup to the Old Firm game – matchday live
items.summary: ⚽ News, discussion and buildup before the day’s action ⚽ Live scores | Tables | Follow us on Bluesky | Mail Daniel Arsenal fan Balaji has messaged in to say: “Should Spurs lose to Fulham or later, Arsenal beat Chelsea, today will mark the St. Totteringham’s Day! Brighton v Nottingham Forest Fulham v Tottenham Manchester United v Crystal Palace Arsenal v Chelsea Continue reading...

items.title: Grim verklaart keuzes voor Gloukh, Klaassen en Weghorst
items.summary: Fred Grim heeft zondag tekst en uitleg gegeven bij zijn opstelling. De Ajax-trainer kiest tegen PEC Zwolle voor Wout Weghorst in de spits en een middenveld met Davy Klaassen en Oscar Gloukh, wat ten koste gaat van Sean Steur.

items.title: VAR ontneemt Burnley 'een van grootste comebacks ooit': 'Het is een puinhoop'
items.summary: Bij Burnley waren de druiven zuur na afloop van het spektakelstuk tegen Brentford. De ploeg van doelpuntenmaker Zian Flemming was dicht bij een waanzinnige comeback, maar zag de VAR roet in het eten gooien. Tot tweemaal toe.

items.title: Oxlade-Chamberlain starts and Schmeichel absent for Celtic at Rangers LIVE!
Opstelling Ajax: Klaassen terug, basisplekken voor Gloukh en Weghorst
items.summary: Fred Grim heeft zijn Ajax-opstelling voor het duel met PEC Zwolle uit de doeken gedaan. Davy Klaassen keert terug op het Amsterdamse middenveld. Kasper Dolberg is niet fit genoeg voor een basisplek en wordt vervangen door Wout Weghorst. Ook Oscar Gloukh speelt.

items.title: Green wins Women's World Championship for second time
items.summary: Australian Hannah Green won the HSBC Women's World Championship for a second time after holding off fast-finishing American Auston Kim to claim a one-stroke victory.

items.title: Woodman’s quiet revolution paying dividends for table-topping Bromley
items.summary: The south-east London club have promotion to League One within sight in just their second season in the EFL with their manager central to the transformation With half an hour to go before kick-off , a roar echoes round the ground. MK Dons have levelled with Cambridge United via a penalty deep into injury time, Aaron Collins scoring from the spot to deny the hosts victory. In the 20-minute interlude between Shayne Lavery’s opener at the Abbey Stadium and the referee’s fateful whistle, Cambridge looked set to go top of the table. Instead Bromley get under way against Accrington Stanley with a one-point lead at the summit of League Two, much to the relief of the home fans. Continue reading...

items.title: A summer season would free women’s football from constraints of men’s game | Suzanne Wrack
items.summary: Breaking from European traditions would bring a TV boost and help build a schedule that works both for players and fans The announcement that Major League Soccer (MLS) is to switch from a summer season to a winter one has reignited the debate about the National Women’s Soccer League’s (NWSL) schedule. This is not a new conversation: the pros and cons of alignment with the European calendar have been considered for many years by the NWSL. Continue reading...

items.title: Navarrete stops Nunez to become unified champion
items.summary: Emanuel Navarrete stopped Eduardo Nunez before the start of the 11th round to unify the WBO and IBF super-featherweight titles in Arizona on Saturday night.

items.title: Nelson: Fury is still the second best heavyweight in the world
items.summary: Tyson Fury remains the second best heavyweight in the world, but he still has come to terms with his loss to Oleksandr Usyk, says former world champion and Sky Sports boxing expert Johnny Nelson...

items.title: Peters says Hull KR 'let their fans down' with massive loss in Las Vegas
items.summary: Hull KR head coach Willie Peters said his side delivered their worst performance under him as they "let their fans down" with a 58-6 loss to Leeds Rhinos in Las Vegas.

items.title: Carrick not interested in personal Glasner duel
items.summary: Manchester United interim boss Michael Carrick comes up against one of the major contenders for the permanent job when Oliver Glasner's Crystal Palace go to Old Trafford on Sunday.

items.title: Free-scoring Semenyo takes burden off Haaland
items.summary: Manchester City have often been accused of being too reliant on Erling Haaland this season. There are signs that could be changing.
